assertions.description: AudioContext is exposed on the global — the surface for the new renderSizeHint option.
BaseAudioContext.prototype exposes a renderQuantumSize attribute — the readable counterpart to renderSizeHint.
A freshly-constructed AudioContext with no options reports renderQuantumSize === 128 frames per spec.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;const c=new Ctx();const ok=c.renderQuantumSize===128;try{await c.close();}catch(_){};return ok;})()

assertions.description: Passing renderSizeHint as an integer is permitted by the AudioContextOptions dictionary; constructor must not throw for a small valid hint.

assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;let c;try{c=new Ctx({renderSizeHint:'default'});}catch(e){return false;}const ok=c.renderQuantumSize===128;try{await c.close();}catch(_){};return ok;})()

assertions.description: renderSizeHint accepts the 'hardware' enumeration value — UA picks a render quantum optimised for the device.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const Ctx=window.AudioContext||window.webkitAudioContext;if(!Ctx)return false;let c;try{c=new Ctx({renderSizeHint:'hardware'});}catch(e){return false;}const q=c.renderQuantumSize;try{await c.close();}catch(_){};return Number.isInteger(q) && q>0;})()

assertions.description: OfflineAudioContext also accepts renderSizeHint in its options dictionary.
